chunk_summary: This section outlines observed feeding problem patterns and targeted interventions during clinic feeding observations, emphasizing posture, pacing, screen-free meals, responsive feeding, texture adjustments, and safety protocols.
learning_objectives: Identify common feeding problem patterns during clinic observations.
Apply targeted interventions to improve feeding posture, pacing, and caregiver strategies.
Recognize safety signs and implement dysphagia evaluation protocols.
Understand when to escalate cases to SLP/OT for advanced feeding skill support.
key_facts: Prioritize upright posture with hips and trunk support, feet stabilization, and head midline for feeding stability.
Coach pacing strategies such as 'one bite, wait for swallow' and smaller boluses to improve coordination.
Screen-free meals with predictable timing and caregiver-led feeding improve appetite and reduce grazing behaviors.
Responsive feeding techniques include acknowledging refusal, pausing, offering choices, and reoffering later without threats or rewards.
Safety signs like coughing, wet voice, or color change require immediate feed cessation, texture simplification, and dysphagia evaluation.
Escalate cases to SLP/OT if meal duration remains excessive or caregiver coaching fails to improve feeding outcomes.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Responsive feeding techniques include acknowledging refusal, pausing, offering choices, and reoffering later without threats or rewards. (UNICEF Infant and Young Child Feeding counselling cards)
